gaps.current_state: The protocol freezes AuthorityOperationProfile semantics, but product-specific transport paths, fraud-header templates, and provider-code mappings still require concrete adapter data.
gaps.required_closure: Instantiate authority product profiles per provider/environment and bind exact provider codes to the canonical response classes.

gaps.current_state: The prose fail-closes weak, ambiguous, and unbound ingress, but it does not fully enumerate the operator workflow for converting weak authority-reference-only evidence into strong lineage proof.
gaps.required_closure: Define the manual investigation and approval workflow for promoting quarantined ingress after stronger evidence is gathered.

gaps.current_state: Section 9.13B defines the reconciliation scoring model and thresholds, but the source-family weighting policy it references lives outside the chapter and is not exported here as concrete per-source numbers.
gaps.required_closure: Bind reconciliation source-family weights to a versioned runtime policy artifact shared with the trust and authority uncertainty layers.

gaps.current_state: The correction cause family is intentionally reserved, but concrete downstream workflow playbooks for each cause enum are not fully enumerated.
gaps.required_closure: Define operator, client-visible, and replay behavior for each authority correction cause before live provider support is enabled.

gaps.current_state: The protocol requires explicit resend_control_reason_codes[] and send_revalidation_reason_codes[], but this chapter does not publish a complete machine-readable enum of those reason codes.
gaps.required_closure: Publish a dedicated authority reason-code registry covering send-time blocks, resend refusals, escalation triggers, and correction causes.
